untranslatable_reason: Signal requires measurement of 'personal fundamental analysis conviction >50%', 'consensus narrative age >1 month', and 'logical causality assessment' — these are qualitative trader judgments and macro-regime variables that cannot be operationalized using price-based technical indicators. The core thesis (trading against consensus when fundamental view contradicts market narrative) is a discretionary meta-strategy requiring external fundamental data, sentiment analysis, and trader conviction scoring not available in the allowed indicator set. No combination of OHLCV, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger Bands, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, or pivot indicators can capture 'consensus belief', 'conviction level', or 'causality strength'.
translation_notes: This signal is fundamentally a discretionary contrarian framework based on fundamental analysis and sentiment divergence, not a technical pattern. It requires: (1) external consensus data (not available), (2) trader conviction scoring (subjective), (3) narrative age tracking (macro-temporal), and (4) causality assessment (qualitative). The parameters provided (conviction_threshold, consensus_age_months) confirm this is a judgment-based strategy. Recommend implementing as a manual trading rule with external data feeds rather than a pure technical backtest.
